accepted_plan.match: The ordered packet explicitly names collision-safe project/worktree creation and selection.
accepted_plan.limit: The plan admits bounded target-native implementation but still requires one claimed packet, target-local proof, and ordinary review.

implementation_receipt.finding: Selection, grouping, recovery, lifecycle actions, and header controls landed, but the receipt explicitly says no renderer path picker or worktree creation logic was introduced and grants no worktree-creation authority.

product_spec.effect: Keep proposal, admission, claim, proof, acceptance, merge, release, and public claim separate; exact dedupe prevents duplicate candidates.
Worktrees are durable engine resources; unchanged cleanup, changed retention, dirty/unpushed refusal, cleanup receipts, and delivery state are required.
The unmet obligation may be surfaced as candidate work through the bounded shared projection, but provider completion is not a target verdict and post-turn revalidation must remain honest.

assurance_spec.effect: The proposed Full Auto AssuranceSpec governs this mission's run evidence, not worktree lifecycle acceptance. A reviewed oracle/falsifier/environment/independent-review design for the worktree candidate must be supplied by the target's assurance path before verified or accepted state.

live_issue_check.conclusion: None owns typed worktree creation. Repository issue policy does not justify manufacturing a feature issue; the accepted T3UI-04 plan is the target-owned ledger.

claim_protocol.required_before_mutation: Record one bounded CLAIM against the accepted packet with exact base, worktree identity, paths, hot files/contracts, verification, and timestamp; confirm no active process/worktree owns it.

implemented: stable opaque project, repository, worktree, WorkContext, and coding-session refs
one private root grant per admitted workspace
missing-worktree recovery with canonical identity retained
project/worktree grouping, selection, filtering, paging, archive, recover, delete confirmation, and header controls
branch list, create, and checkout through the existing Git host
residual: no typed host intent creates a worktree from an explicit base
no target proof covers path collision refusal or independent worktree progress
no proof establishes exact worktree-root propagation through Git, terminal, files, diff, and tools
no reviewed proof covers restart-safe cleanup without deleting changed, dirty, unpushed, or owner data
capability E3 remains partial because creation is agent-driven Bash

proposal.title: T3UI-04 residual — typed, collision-safe worktree creation and conservative lifecycle
proposal.bounded_outcome: Add one main-process-owned typed worktree creation path that requires an explicit repository and base ref, chooses or validates a unique private path, creates the Git worktree, registers canonical worktree/session/WorkContext refs, and projects only bounded identity to the renderer. Preserve the resource across restart and implement conservative cleanup outcomes required by Desktop AC-6.
hot_contracts: Git mutation serialization and cleanup receipt vocabulary

source_derived: require an explicit base and fail closed when it is absent
publish authoritative worktree identity before execution
route every work surface through exact worktree identity
serialize conflicting mutations per repository/worktree key
treat parallelism as isolated roots rather than concurrent mutation of one checkout
target_native: main process owns Git and filesystem effects; renderer sends schema-decoded intent only
raw local paths remain private and never enter public-safe projections or receipts
reuse canonical catalog, grant, WorkContext, and coding-session identities
creation is idempotent or refuses with a typed collision; no silent adoption of an unrelated directory
cleanup auto-removes only unchanged worktrees, retains changed worktrees, and refuses dirty or unpushed state with a typed receipt
never delete owner data, the repository root, the current worktree, or a path outside the validated worktree authority
do not add credentials, remote placement, release, deployment, or public parity claims
explicit_rejections: do not copy T3 service or client-local scheduler architecture
do not expose a renderer shell, arbitrary path, or raw Git command surface
do not treat a selected directory as proof it is the registered worktree
do not infer cleanup safety from a successful turn or clean catalog projection
do not close the candidate on fixture tests alone or on provider self-report

dependencies_and_collisions.dependency: current accepted T3UI-04 packet and target ProductSpec remain unchanged
reviewed AssuranceSpec-style proof design for Desktop AC-6 and creation safety
dependencies_and_collisions.effect: implementation may produce candidate evidence but cannot self-verify or self-accept

dependencies_and_collisions.dependency: one exact CLAIM and integration owner for main IPC, catalog schemas, Git serialization, and cleanup receipts
rebase/reconcile from pinned target to current origin/main
dependencies_and_collisions.effect: refresh or supersede this proposal if authority, code, issue, or claim state changed

proposed_acceptance: Creation without an explicit base refuses before any filesystem or Git mutation.
Two creation requests for the same target identity/path deterministically collapse or one receives a typed collision; no partial catalog row remains.
Two distinct worktrees can execute independently while conflicting operations against one repository/worktree key serialize or refuse.
Git, terminal, files, diff, review, and tool launches resolve the same canonical WorkContext root and cannot accept a renderer-supplied replacement path.
Restart reloads the same canonical worktree/session/WorkContext refs; a missing directory enters explicit recovery rather than silent recreation.
Unchanged worktrees may be removed with a cleanup receipt; changed worktrees are retained; dirty or unpushed worktrees refuse cleanup; owner data is never recursively deleted.
Renderer and public-safe receipts expose bounded refs/status only and contain no raw local path, command, prompt, credential, or provider-private state.
Capability E3 changes from partial only after target-local proof, reviewed evidence, and target authority support that exact claim.

oracles: real temporary Git repository with two actual linked worktrees and exact HEAD/base/path inspection
catalog snapshot and persisted restart reload decoded through production schemas
cleanup receipt and on-disk postcondition inspection
falsifiers: inject an absent base, occupied path, duplicate request, Git failure after directory creation, mismatched catalog root, dirty tree, unpushed commit, missing directory, restart boundary, and cleanup interruption
mutate a fixture to bypass collision or dirty/unpushed refusal and require the proof to fail
proof_plan_candidate.environment: OS-temporary directories only; no owner repository, source checkout, credentials, network, Keychain, release, signing, or deployment
proof_plan_candidate.independent_review: A reviewer other than the implementation producer inspects the proof design, receipt, destructive-path guards, and exact diff before any verified or accepted disposition.
evidence_requirements: test counts and named adversarial cases
public-safe creation and cleanup receipt digests

close_rule.evidence_present: Implementation diff and raw target-local results exist at exact refs; this does not imply verification.
close_rule.implemented: The bounded typed creation/lifecycle path is merged into a candidate branch or commit; this does not imply proof passed.
close_rule.verified: A reviewed proof design executes in the bound environment, every oracle and falsifier passes, and evidence digests are independently checked.
close_rule.owner_accepted: The owner or delegated target authority explicitly accepts the verified result; provider or implementer completion cannot supply this state.
close_rule.merged: The reviewed commit is integrated into current main with claim release and no unresolved collision.
close_rule.released: A separately authorized packaged release includes the merged change; this proposal grants no release authority.
close_rule.public_claim: A separately authorized promise/public-claim gate approves exact wording; no T3 parity claim is implied.
close_rule.terminal_no_change: If current target reconciliation shows the exact behavior and proof already landed, close as no_material_delta with exact evidence rather than duplicate implementation.
close_rule.terminal_rejected_or_superseded: Persist the target authority and reason; unchanged evidence cannot reopen the candidate.

sibling_dispositions.exception: target-native ProviderLane architecture; real-run and independent assurance remain separate evidence gates
Full Auto snapshot publication is intentionally fail-soft and no event-reducer requirement is admitted
exact peer profiles and bounded duplex transport exist; arbitrary ACP parity is not claimed
fleet-wide rollout is deferred without a current packet; rejected T3 credential authority stays rejected
